channel.title: ICYMI Law — AI Paper-like Links
channel.description: Popular AI paper-like links shared recently on Bluesky.

item.title: Students still think they write better than GenAI, but not equally: Disciplinary differences in usage, trust, and writing practices with artificial intelligence
item.description:
This survey study (n=342) identifies undergraduates’ GenAI usage patterns, their attitudes of trust towards these technologies, and their self-reported positive

🔥🔥🔥🔥 · reposts: 15 · likes: 35

item.title: Thinking—Fast, Slow, and Artificial: How AI is Reshaping Human Reasoning and the Rise of Cognitive Surrender
item.description:
People increasingly consult generative artificial intelligence (AI) while reasoning. As AI becomes embedded in daily thought, what becomes of human judgment? We

🔥 · reposts: 6 · likes: 17

item.title: SatLM: Satisfiability-Aided Language Models Using Declarative Prompting
item.description:
Prior work has combined chain-of-thought prompting in large language models (LLMs) with programmatic representations to perform effective and transparent reasoning. While such an approach works well f...

reposts: 0 · likes: 8

item.title: Retrieval Collapses When AI Pollutes the Web
item.description:
The rapid proliferation of AI-generated content on the Web presents a structural risk to information retrieval, as search engines and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems increasingly consume ...

reposts: 0 · likes: 4

item.title: Critical Confabulation: Can LLMs Hallucinate for Social Good?
item.description:
LLMs hallucinate, yet some confabulations can have social affordances if carefully bounded. We propose critical confabulation (inspired by critical fabulation from literary and social theory), the use...

reposts: 1 · likes: 2
